$parserComment: plan-24 Stage 7 cap lift. `maxExpressionDepth` is the Pratt walker's expression-NESTING cap -- the threshold of the positioned, fail-loud `P_ExpressionTooDeep` backstop -- now 100% config-driven: the loader reads this key into `ParserConfig::maxExpressionDepth` via `parserConfigFor` (src/analysis/compilation_unit/compilation_unit.cpp); the C++ field default (256) is only the fallback when this key is absent. RAISED 256 -> 1024 (4x). WHY 1024 and not higher: plan-24 flattened the parser's operator/ternary/prefix/infix descent and ALL of cst_to_hir/hir_to_mir/semantic/const-eval onto explicit work-stacks (O(1) host-stack per nesting level), so operator/ternary chains are now bounded ONLY by this cap. The ONE residual recursion is the parser's paren/postfix-body arm (the deferred Stage 5b), which still costs one host frame per nested '(' on the 64 MiB deep-recursion worker stack (core/substrate/large_stack_call `kDeepRecursionStackBytes`). The cap is a SAFETY BACKSTOP: it must trip BEFORE that arm overflows on the WORST supported build, otherwise a deep paren nest would crash (rc 127) instead of failing loud -- so the cap must sit BELOW the tightest crash ceiling, not just near it. MEASURED native-Windows paren-crash ceilings (deepest '(' nest that still compiled on the 64 MiB worker): MSVC Debug ~3000-3200, MSVC Release ~16000-16500, MinGW Release ~20000-24000. 1024 leaves ~3x headroom below the tightest (MSVC Debug) ceiling -- covering Debug-build frame bloat plus cross-platform (Linux/macOS) frame variance -- while being 4x the old 256 and far above any realistic machine-generated C nesting. A nest beyond 1024 still FAILS LOUD with the positioned diagnostic + recovery; it never crashes. toy/tsql omit the `parser` block, so they keep the 256 fallback.
$preprocessComment: FC13: opts c-subset into the config-SELECTED C preprocessor pass (src/analysis/preprocess/). `enabled:true` runs the pass between tokenize and parse: it builds ONE synthesized buffer by recursively splicing quote-#include"h" headers' text, tokenizes once, then runs the object-like macro pass (#define/#undef, expansion + rescan + blue-paint self-ref guard, directive removal). The TOKEN-name fields (directiveIntroToken/quoteIncludeToken/angleIncludeToken) are validated against the interned token kinds; the directive-keyword strings (define/undef/include) are matched by lexeme TEXT against the token after `#` (define/undef are NOT grammar keywords -- they lex as Identifier). Angle #include <h> is LEFT in place for the existing post-parse FF11 import resolver (so angleIncludeToken lets the PP RECOGNISE and pass it through). toy/tsql-subset declare NO preprocess block -> their pass is a strict identity (token stream in == out).

preprocess.$variadicCommaElisionComment: FC15 paste residuals (D-PP-VARIADIC-GNU-COMMA-ELISION): opt into the GNU `,##__VA_ARGS__` extension. `sep ## __VA_ARGS__` with an EMPTY variadic part drops the preceding separator (`LOG("x")` -> `f("x")`); non-empty keeps it. Without this flag, standard C placemarker behavior leaves the separator. The separator/__VA_ARGS__ are matched by the config KIND/name above, never hardcoded.
preprocess.$predefinedMacrosComment: FC15b (C 6.10.8): the predefined macros. Each entry names the macro IDENTIFIER + a materialization `kind`; the engine keys EVERY behavior off `kind`, never the name. `line`/`file` are OFFSET-derived (resolved through the line-map against the INVOCATION offset -> the invocation line / the presumed file name); `date`/`time` are computed once at preprocess construction (`"Mmm dd yyyy"` / `"hh:mm:ss"`); `constant` carries a static `value` spelling verbatim. `#define`/`#undef` of any of these is a constraint violation (C 6.10.8.1) -> fail loud. __STDC_VERSION__ = 202311L (C23). c95 (Windows-host sub-arc): the OPTIONAL `availableObjectFormats` per-entry filter (mirrors the shipped-lib descriptor field; EMPTY = every format, a non-empty set of object-format NAMES restricts the macro to those formats — validated against objectFormatKindFromName at load, an unknown name fails loud) lets the Windows-selection macros be predefined for the `pe` target ONLY: `_WIN32`/`_WIN64` (value 1) make sqlite's amalgamation select os_win.c (SQLITE_OS_WIN); `__stdcall`/`__cdecl`/`__fastcall`/`WINAPI` ERASE to nothing (empty value) since PE64 has a single MS-x64 calling convention and imports are un-decorated (a `__stdcall`-qualified declarator then parses on pe, stays an ordinary identifier off-pe). None leak onto elf/macho. The 7 core C 6.10.8 macros carry NO filter (available everywhere, unchanged).
preprocess.$fc15cComment: FC15c. `#pragma` (C 6.10.6): consumed-and-DROPPED (C 6.10.6p2 licenses ignoring an unrecognized pragma; DSS recognizes none). `__has_include` (C23 6.10.1p4): tests header existence with the SAME resolution `#include` uses -- the angle delimiters are matched by TOKEN KIND (`hasIncludeAngleOpenToken`=LtOp / `hasIncludeAngleCloseToken`=GtOp), never the `<`/`>` bytes (agnosticism); the angle form maps `<stem>.json` on the system path (DSS ships JSON descriptors, e.g. `stdio.json`) so `__has_include(<stdio.h>)` agrees with `#include <stdio.h>`. `__has_c_attribute` (C23 6.10.1p4): reports the version int of a known standard attribute or 0; the lookup ignores leading/trailing `__` (so `__deprecated__` matches `deprecated`). The C23 standard attributes are 202311.

directive.$comment: FC13: the line-scoped C-directive mode. `#` pushes it (popAtNewline -> auto-pops at the next newline so a malformed directive never leaks state into the following line). It is an INLINE override table that lists ONLY `include`; the tokenizer falls back to the GLOBAL table for every other lexeme, so `define`/`undef`, `"..."`, identifiers AND operators all lex normally. CRUCIALLY there is NO `<`-override here: a `<` inside a NON-include directive (e.g. `#define X 1<<2`) falls back to the global `<<`/`<` operators -- no longer catastrophically mis-lexed. The `include` override re-emits IncludeKeyword AND arms the header context (replaceMode -> header-context) so the FOLLOWING `<h>` opens a header path. Scoping the mode-switch to THIS mode (rather than to the global `include` keyword) means a bare `include` identifier at main depth never triggers a replaceMode (which would abort at main-mode depth) -- it just lexes as IncludeKeyword with no side-effect.

header-context.$comment: FC13: armed by the `include` keyword (replaceMode). An INLINE override table lists ONLY `<`; the tokenizer falls back to the GLOBAL table for every other lexeme, so `"h"` still opens the quote-string body and identifiers lex normally. The ONE override is `<` -> HeaderStart (replaceMode -> header-body, stringStyle.endsAt `>`). The override-first longest-match rule means `<` wins at length 1 and the global `<<`/`<=` are deliberately suppressed inside an include header context -- correct, since `#include <<x>>` is not valid C. popAtNewline guards a malformed `#include` with no header.

$fc2CastExprComment: FC2 Part A: C-style cast `(type)expr` (castExpr/castTypeRef/castTypeBase/castOperand shapes). castExpr shares the `( ... )` opener with compoundLiteralExpr/parenExpr inside the speculative operand alt; the probe disambiguates structurally (a brace-init follows the compound literal's `)`, an expression follows the cast's). `commitRequiresTypeName` names the type-position child rule: a structurally-successful probe COMMITS only per the parser's GENERIC type-name triage — (1) a type that is NOT a lone Identifier (keyword/struct-tag/star/const present) always commits, those forms cannot be expressions; (2) a lone Identifier the binder sketch knows as a TYPE commits; (3) one it knows as a VALUE rolls back to the value reading (shadowing-aware); (4) an UNKNOWN lone Identifier commits iff the token after the type's closer is NOT an infix/postfix/ternary operator per the operator table (the value reading could not continue), else rolls back AND records an AmbiguousTypeNameCandidate for the compilation-unit oracle's one-shot reparse (cross-file typedefs). castTypeRef duplicates typeRefAllowingStruct PLUS a bare-Identifier base (typedef names in cast position). NOTE (FC7 D-FC7-NESTED-STRUCT-FIELD): typeRefAllowingStruct ITSELF now also carries the bare-Identifier alt — but it is consumed ONLY by structField/unionField, and a struct/union body has NO expression-statement production, so the C decl-vs-expr ambiguity (`T x;` vs `a * b;`) cannot arise there (a bare name in field-type position is unambiguously a type; a non-type fails loud at semantic scope-lookup). varDecl/param do NOT use typeRefAllowingStruct — they use declHead/declHeadForParam with the binder-sketch `commitRequiresTypeName` commit — so the statement/param decl-vs-expr triage is untouched by that alt. castOperand is a second expr-entry rule re-entering the Pratt walker at PREFIX precedence (90): the cast operand binds prefix + postfix operators but NOT binary/ternary ones, giving C-correct `(int)a + b` == ((int)a)+b, `(int)x[2]` == (int)(x[2]), `-(int)x`, and `(int)(long)x` chains. Same wrapper vocabulary as `expression` (wrapper rule names are per-schema; the loader dedups).
$ff11AngleIncludeComment: FF11 angle-include lexing (FC13 mode structure). `#` pushes the line-scoped `directive` mode (popAtNewline:true -> auto-pops at the next newline, so a malformed `#include` -- or any directive -- never leaks state into the following line; C directives are line-oriented). CRUCIALLY `directive` no longer overrides `<`: the ONLY override there is `include` (-> IncludeKeyword + replaceMode to `header-context`), so a `<` in a NON-include directive (e.g. `#define X 1<<2`) falls back to the global `<<`/`<` operators -- no longer mis-lexed as a header opener (the headline FC13 lexer fix). The `<`-OPENS-a-header-path override moved DOWNSTREAM to `header-context` (armed by `include`): there `<` -> HeaderStart (replaceMode -> header-body, stringStyle.endsAt `>`), while `"h"` still opens the quote-string body. The override-first longest-match rule (language-config-spec section 9.6) makes `<` win at length 1 inside `header-context` so `<<`/`<=` are suppressed THERE only -- correct, since `#include <<x>>` is not valid C. The `header-body` mode mirrors the `string` body: a coalesced `HeaderPath` captures the raw path bytes between `<` and the closing `>` (which pops back to main); escapeKind:none. The quote form keeps the global `"`->StringStart->string body unchanged (both `#include <h>` and `#include "h"` work). The resolver reads the raw path slice (a filename, not a decoded string) for both forms, and picks the search path by which token kind it finds under the directive (imports.pathToken=StringStart -> self-dir+includeDirs; imports.systemPathToken=HeaderStart -> shippedLibDirs).

//.$endsAtExclusiveComment: c22 (D-PP-LINE-COMMENT-BEFORE-DIRECTIVE): a `//` comment ends AT the newline but EXCLUDES it — the newline must survive as its own Newline token, else a directive on the next line after `code // comment` loses its line boundary and the preprocessor fails to recognize it (firstOnLine sees the comment-consumed newline as a body char, not a boundary).

topLevel.$comment: NON-speculative: each alt has a disjoint FIRST. struct/union/enum heads route to topLevelDecl (via topLevelHead's body/ref specifier forms) — the bare top-level structDecl/unionDecl/enumDecl rules were folded into topLevelDecl (optional declarator) so a `struct` lead has ONE owner here and only topLevelHead's single-level speculation runs (the two-level topLevel-speculation path was unsound — D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION). Those three now-unreferenced rules (+ their semantics/hirKind/scopes rows) were DELETED in the closing cycle (no dead config); the shared structField/unionField/enumerator helpers stay (the *SpecifierBody forms consume them). c81: `emptyStmt` (a lone `;`) is admitted as an external declaration — sqlite shell.c's `SQLITE_EXTENSION_INIT1;` expands EMPTY in the static-shell build, leaving a stray file-scope semicolon (x2, base64.c/base85.c sections) that gcc/clang accept (-pedantic warns; C 6.9 formally disallows it, but accepting it is the universal implementation posture). REUSES the statement-position `emptyStmt` rule exactly as c30 reuses `typedefDecl` in both positions: FIRST = {EndStatement}, disjoint from every other alt -> unique-production direct descent, still non-speculative; its existing `hirKind: Skip` row lowers it to nothing in BOTH positions (the includeDirective->Skip top-level precedent), so no semantics/scopes row is needed.

structField.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: a struct/union field is a type-specifier HEAD + an OPTIONAL `structMemberDeclaratorList` (a comma-separated list of per-slot declarators sharing the head base type, e.g. `int *a, b[4], *c;` — C 6.7.2.1) + `EndStatement`. SUPERSEDES c10's single `{optional declarator}{optional bitfieldDeclSuffix}`: each slot (`structMemberDeclarator`) now holds its OWN `declarator` AND its OWN `bitfieldDeclSuffix` so `int a:3, b:5;` resolves each width per-slot. Routing the member through the SAME `declarator` every other position uses (params/locals/globals) makes a function-pointer member (`int (*f)(int);`) parse + type-derive identically; the array suffix (`int arr[3];`) lives inside the declarator's OWN directDeclarator suffix chain. The LIST is OPTIONAL to admit the `int ;` declares-nothing form; a single-slot list with absent declarator + present suffix is the anonymous bit-field (`int : 3;` / zero-width `int : 0;`); the over-admitted nameless non-bitfield forms FAIL LOUD at semantic composition (anonymous member must be a bit-field).

bitfieldDeclSuffix.$comment: FC8 D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD: the `: width` suffix; the width is a constant-expression evaluated + range-checked against the field's integer type at semantic.

structMemberDeclarator.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: ONE slot of a struct/union member list — an OPTIONAL `declarator` (its OWN pointer/array/fn suffixes; the `*` binds PER-SLOT so `int *a, b;` is Ptr<int> then int) + an OPTIONAL `bitfieldDeclSuffix` (now INSIDE the slot so `int a:3, b:5;` resolves widths independently). The declarator is optional to admit an anonymous bit-field (`int : 3;` — absent declarator, present suffix). The engine's `memberDeclaratorRule` role descends to the inner `declarator` for name + type derivation.

structMemberDeclaratorList.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: `structMemberDeclarator (, structMemberDeclarator)*` — mirrors `initDeclaratorList` for struct/union members. The engine's `memberListRule` role iterates the slot children (commas skipped).

structTypeRef.$comment: c25: the REF-ONLY struct specifier, kept for the param/cast positions (declHeadForParam, castTypeBase) where a struct type is a pure tag reference. The DECLARATION-head positions no longer use this pair-with-body form — they route through the UNIFIED `structSpec` (below), which folds body-vs-ref into ONE rule so it is the SOLE candidate for `struct` at those heads → the parser's unique-production direct descent parses the member body NON-speculatively (no probe, no token budget — the c25 fix for `struct sqlite3`).

structSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the UNIFIED struct specifier (C 6.7.2.1 struct-or-union-specifier) — `struct {opt tag} {opt body}`. ONE rule for BOTH the type-DEFINITION form (`struct P { … }` — a `structBody` child is present) and the bare tag-REFERENCE form (`struct P` — absent). Replaces the former `structSpecifierBody | structTypeRef` SPECULATIVE pair in the 4 body-bearing declaration-head alts (topLevelCompositeSpec, typeSpecifierForDecl, typedefHead, typeBaseAllowingStruct). Because it is now the SOLE candidate keyed on StructKeyword in those alts, the parser's LL(k) unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (parser.cpp ~1804-1844) dispatches it NON-speculatively — the member list parses with NO speculation budget, so an arbitrarily large body (`struct sqlite3`) never trips the 4096-token probe cliff. The body-vs-ref split becomes an INTERNAL 1-token (`BlockOpen`) decision via the `{optional structBody}`, also non-speculative. The binder is DUAL-MODE on the SAME node (semantics: `definesWhenChild: structBody`): body present ⇒ the EXACT former `structSpecifierBody` define path (mint type, bind tag, open scope, compose fields from the `structBody` subtree); body absent ⇒ the EXACT former `structTypeRef` tag-reference path (isTagReference). FOLLOW-independent (the rejected D2): no prune of the ref candidate is needed because there IS no separate ref candidate here.

structBody.$comment: c25: the brace-delimited struct member list, factored OUT of `structSpec` so its PRESENCE is the define-vs-reference discriminator (the binder's `definesWhenChild`). NOT a scope rule itself: the fields bind into the scope opened by the enclosing `structSpec` (the analyzer's Pass-1.5 field collection walks that scope, not direct children, so the extra nesting level is transparent). Member shape identical to the former `structSpecifierBody` tail.

unionField.$comment: c23 D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR: a union field mirrors `structField` — a type-specifier HEAD + an OPTIONAL `structMemberDeclaratorList` (per-slot declarators sharing the head, e.g. `int *a, b;`) + `EndStatement`. The per-slot `structMemberDeclarator` holds its own declarator (function-pointer-member support: `int (*f)(int);`) AND its own bitfield suffix. The array suffix lives inside the declarator's directDeclarator suffix chain.

unionTypeRef.$comment: c25: the REF-ONLY union specifier, kept for the param/cast positions (declHeadForParam, castTypeBase). The declaration-head positions route through the UNIFIED `unionSpec` (below). Mirrors structTypeRef.

unionSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the UNIFIED union specifier — `union {opt tag} {opt body}`. Mirrors `structSpec`: ONE rule for define (`union U { … }`) and reference (`union U`), the SOLE UnionKeyword candidate in the 4 body-bearing alts → non-speculative direct descent (no member-body probe budget). Dual-mode binder via `definesWhenChild: unionBody`.

unionBody.$comment: c25: the brace-delimited union member list, factored out of `unionSpec` as the define-vs-reference discriminator. NOT a scope rule (fields bind into the enclosing unionSpec's scope). Member shape identical to the former `unionSpecifierBody` tail.

enumTypeRef.$comment: c25: the REF-ONLY enum specifier, kept for the param/cast positions (declHeadForParam, castTypeBase). The declaration-head positions route through the UNIFIED `enumSpec` (below). Mirrors structTypeRef.

enumSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the UNIFIED enum specifier — `enum {opt tag} {opt body}`. Mirrors `structSpec`: ONE rule for define (`enum E { A, B }`) and reference (`enum E`), the SOLE EnumKeyword candidate in the 4 body-bearing alts → non-speculative direct descent. Dual-mode binder via `definesWhenChild: enumBody`. The enumerator-list (with trailing comma) lives in `enumBody`.

enumBody.$comment: c25: the brace-delimited enumerator list, factored out of `enumSpec` as the define-vs-reference discriminator. NOT a scope rule (enumerators bind into the enclosing enumSpec's scope, then lift to the enclosing namespace scope per fieldChildren.liftToEnclosingScope). Enumerator-list shape (optional list, trailing comma) identical to the former `enumSpecifierBody` tail.

topLevelDecl.$comment: C declaration = decl-specifiers init-declarator-list? `;` (or a function-definition block tail). The init-declarator-list is OPTIONAL: a bare `struct P { … };` / `enum E { … };` is a type-only declaration (head introduces the tag, no object declared) — the semantic tier mints the type from the head and emits a TypeDecl; a head that introduces NO tag with no declarator (`int ;`) is a C6.7p2 'declares nothing' constraint violation (fail-loud). A trailing declarator (`struct P { … } v;`) or a block tail (`struct Tri mkTri(void){…}`) takes the normal global/function path.

topLevelHead.$comment: Top-level head: a keyword type specifier (`typeSpecifierSeq`, e.g. `int`/`unsigned long`), a struct/union/enum composite specifier (`topLevelCompositeSpec` — the SPECULATIVE body-vs-ref alt), OR a lone Identifier (typedef name). The OUTER alt is NON-speculative: FIRST(typeSpecifierSeq)={type keywords}, FIRST(topLevelCompositeSpec)={struct,union,enum}, FIRST(Identifier)={ident} are pairwise disjoint — so a plain `int x;` routes DIRECTLY to typeSpecifierSeq (NO extra wrapper node, preserving the pre-existing tree shape) and only a struct/union/enum lead enters the single-level speculation that closes D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION. topLevel stays non-speculative: `struct` routes unambiguously to topLevelDecl.

topLevelCompositeSpec.$comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the struct/union/enum SPECIFIER at a top-level declaration head — now ONE unified rule per keyword (`structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec`), each covering BOTH the body form (`struct P { … }`) AND the bare tag reference (`struct P`) via an internal `{optional body}`. The three candidates have pairwise-DISJOINT 1-token FIRSTs (StructKeyword/UnionKeyword/EnumKeyword), so the predictive prune leaves EXACTLY ONE survivor for any composite lead → the parser's unique-production DIRECT DESCENT (parser.cpp ~1804-1844) dispatches it NON-speculatively. The member body therefore parses with NO speculation budget — the c25 fix for `struct sqlite3`'s large body (the former body-vs-ref probe budget-capped at 4096 tokens). `speculative`/`lookahead` are retained but moot under the unique-survivor direct descent (kept so a future non-disjoint addition still has a probe budget). The enum body's trailing-comma enumerator-list parses cleanly (the schema compiler propagates nested-nullable FOLLOW via recomputeAltExpectedSets).

topLevelDeclTail.$comment: FIRST(block)={BlockOpen} vs {EndStatement} — disjoint. A block tail makes the FIRST init-declarator a function DEFINITION (semantic-tier checks enforce: exactly one declarator, named, fn-suffix-bearing, no initializer); an EndStatement tail makes every declarator a global variable.

attrSpec.$comment: FC4 c1: the argument grew the parenthesized-string form `visibility("hidden")` — an Identifier optionally followed by `( string-literal )`. The inline sequences add NO wrapper nodes, so the bare `weak`/`static` tree shapes are byte-identical to pre-FC4. linkageFrom's composite-key lookup (`visibility:hidden`) pairs the identifier with the decoded string body.

stdAttr.$comment: C23 [[...]] attribute specifier (D14: parsed, semantically IGNORED — linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules skips the whole subtree in the linkage scan). Namespaced `[[gnu::attr]]` and a parenthesized string argument are accepted; richer argument forms are residue.

declarator.$comment: alt of (pointer-led, direct-led): NON-nullable (at least one star or a direct), so `int = 5;` / `int;` stay parse errors; the pointer-led branch's OPTIONAL direct admits the abstract star-only form (`int *` in param position). The branch sequences are INLINE so pointerLayer/directDeclarator land as DIRECT children — the shape the engine's declarators roles describe.

directDeclarator.$comment: Base alt is INLINE + SPECULATIVE: fnSuffix vs parenDeclarator genuinely collide on `(` (C 6.7.6.3p11) — fnSuffix probes FIRST (declared order) under its requireKnownType guard; rollback yields the parenthesized-declarator reading. Identifier rides the token-leaf route (no probe); arrayDeclSuffix at base is the abstract `int [3]` param form. lookahead 64 = the operand-alt precedent (1024-token probe budget): a probe must swallow a whole parenthesized param list / nested declarator.

abstractDirectDeclarator.$comment: c26 D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME: the abstract twin of `directDeclarator` for the CAST/sizeof/compound/va_arg type-name tail (C 6.7.7 direct-abstract-declarator). The BASE alt is deliberately ONLY `{fnSuffix, parenDeclarator}` — NOT the full `directDeclarator` base — for two reasons. (1) `Identifier` EXCLUDED: a bare-identifier base would make `(c * c)` (a pervasive multiplication) speculatively commit as a cast `(c *<abstract-decl c>)` — the type child gains >1 leaf so the commit triage's rule-1 'not a lone identifier' fires. (2) `arrayDeclSuffix` EXCLUDED at the BASE (the bare `[N]` form): `(b[0])` (an array-INDEX value expression) would likewise mis-commit as a type-name `b[0]` (base `b` + array suffix). C's direct-abstract-declarator allows a bare `[N]` base, but it collides with the index expression under speculative type-vs-value triage — so the BARE-array type-name (`(int[10])` / `sizeof(int[10])`) is the NAMED LIMITATION D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-ARRAY-TYPE-NAME (off the c26 fn-ptr frontier; the `(*)[N]` array-OF-pointer form still works — its base is `parenDeclarator`, the `[N]` rides the suffix repeat). The remaining base forms — `parenDeclarator` (`(*)`/`(*p)` — a nested NAME still parses and is rejected loud at the type-resolve), `fnSuffix` — REUSE the existing declarator sub-rules verbatim, so the SHARED semantic `directDeclaredType` folds it (`(*)(void)`→Ptr<Fn(void)->int>) identically to `directDeclarator`. The suffix repeat KEEPS `arrayDeclSuffix` so `(*)[N]` works. Speculative base (fnSuffix vs parenDeclarator collide on `(`, exactly as directDeclarator).

fnSuffix.$comment: The commit guard fires ONLY when fnSuffix is probed speculatively (the directDeclarator BASE position); the post-base suffix position dispatches non-speculatively on `(` and never consults it. requireKnownType = C 6.7.6.3p11: a lone unknown identifier between the parens rolls back to the parenthesized-declarator reading AND records the ambiguous-type-name candidate for the CU oracle's seeded reparse.

typeSpecifierForDecl.$comment: KEYWORD/composite-led type specifiers for declaration heads — deliberately NO Identifier arm (the typedef-name head is the ambiguous form, routed through identDeclHead/declOrExprStmt or the committed post-specifier/post-qualifier heads). SPECULATIVE (mirror typedefHead, D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION): admits the struct/union/enum BODY forms so a body-defined LOCAL (`struct P { int a; } v;` as a statement) parses; body forms probe FIRST, a ref with no `{` rolls back to the `*TypeRef` form. Single-level.

declHead.$comment: The COMMITTED any-form head: reached only after a leading storage-class specifier run (`register T x;`) — the statement is already known to be a declaration, so the Identifier (typedef-name) arm is unambiguous here.

kwDeclHead.$comment: The keyword-or-qualifier-led head (the non-speculative varDecl statement branch). Qualifier-led admits the Identifier (typedef) base — `const T x;` is committed by the `const` (no expression starts with a qualifier). Keyword-led is the plain `int x;` form. FIRSTs {Const,Volatile} vs type-keywords/struct/union/enum — disjoint.

identDeclHead.$comment: The AMBIGUOUS bare-typedef-name head (`T x;` vs the expression `T(x)`/`a*b`): lone Identifier + trailing qualifiers only. Leading qualifiers/specifiers route to kwDeclHead/varDecl instead (any of them commits a declaration).

typedefHead.$comment: SPECULATIVE: the struct/union/enum BODY forms collide with the ref forms on their lead keyword — body forms probe FIRST (declared order); `typedef struct P MyP;` rolls back to structTypeRef when no `{` follows. lookahead 256 (4096-token budget): a body probe must swallow the whole member list.

initValue.$comment: FC5: assignmentExpr (NOT expression) — the comma-gate. Covers ALL three initializer consumers (initDeclarator, structField, braceInitList initElement), so `int a = b, c;` stays 2 declarators (not `a = (b,c)`).

typeSpecifierSeq.$comment: FC3 c1: C 6.7.2 type-specifier run — one-or-more specifier KEYWORDS in any order (`unsigned long long int` == `long long unsigned int` == `long unsigned long`). The inner alt is INLINE (no named wrapper rule) so the matched keyword TOKENS land as typeSpecifierSeq's DIRECT children — the semantic engine's multiset arm requires all-token children. Which multisets are VALID (and what core each resolves to, per data model) is the semantics.typeSpecifiers table; invalid combos (`unsigned float`, `short long`) parse fine here and reject SEMANTICALLY by absence from the table (S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination). The seq SUBSUMES the former single-token Void/Int/Long/Char alts (a one-keyword run). Keyword-led, so castExpr's commitRequiresTypeName triage rule 1 (not-a-lone-identifier) always commits a seq-led cast type.

typeBaseAllowingStruct.$comment: FC7 D-FC7-NESTED-STRUCT-FIELD: the trailing `Identifier` alt admits a typedef-name (user struct/union type) as a struct/union FIELD type — symmetric with `typeBase` above, which already carries it. A struct/union body has no expression-statement context, so a bare Identifier in field-type position is UNAMBIGUOUSLY a type-name (no binder-sketch guard needed); a non-type name fails loud at semantic scope-lookup. Tried last, so keyword/tag/enum forms keep priority. D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-BODY-VARDECL-POSITION: also admits the struct/union/enum BODY forms (an inline nested definition `struct Inner { int x; } in;` as a field) — SPECULATIVE (mirror typedefHead), body-before-ref. This makes the body-in-head form RECURSIVE: a structSpecifierBody contains structFields, each of which may itself BE a structSpecifierBody (arbitrary nesting), the recursion falling out of the grammar — single-level speculation at each structField, never nested.

typeRefAllowingStruct.$comment: c23 (D-CSUBSET-STRUCT-MULTI-DECLARATOR): the struct/union member-head TYPE-SPECIFIER (base type + cv-qualifiers ONLY — NO pointer stars). The head qualifier slot is `{repeat headQualifier}` (= const|volatile) so a member head admits `volatile int isInterrupted;` (the SQLite case) and `const volatile`; mirrors the head-qualifier slots on topLevelHead/declHead/kwDeclHead. ★ The prior `{repeat StarOp}` slot was REMOVED: with the c23 member-declarator LIST, pointer stars MUST bind PER-DECLARATOR (inside each slot's `declarator` pointerLayer) — a head star applies to ALL declarators, so `int *a, b;` would wrongly type b as a pointer too (the silent layout miscompile c23 fixes). Each slot's `declarator` now owns its stars, exactly like every other declaration position (params/locals/globals). A head-position volatile FOLLOWED by a declarator `*` is a pointer-to-volatile-POINTEE — still rejected loud S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported by the per-declarator typing arm (head volatile + declarator star), so the reject stays complete. The trailing `{optional ConstKeyword}` is east const on the BASE (`int const`, west of any declarator star); east const on a pointer (`int * const`) rides the declarator's ptrQualifier.

varDecl.$comment: The COMMITTED local declaration statement — its FIRST set has NO Identifier (the bare-typedef-name form lives in declOrExprStmt). Branch 1: storage-class-specifier-led (`register T x;` — the specifier commits the decl, so the head admits the typedef-name arm); branch 2: qualifier-or-keyword-led (`const T x;` / `int x;`). Inline branches keep the children flat: [localDeclSpecifiers?, head, initDeclaratorList?, ;] — the row's specifierPrefix strip makes head=0/list=1 stable.
varDecl.$c28Comment: c28 D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPE-DEFINITION: the `initDeclaratorList` is OPTIONAL — mirroring the top-level `topLevelDecl` (init-declarator-list?). A bare BLOCK-SCOPED struct/union/enum DEFINITION with no declarator (`struct Sublist { int nList; ht_slot *aList; };` as a STATEMENT inside a function — sqlite3.c:68508 walMergesort) routes through the kwDeclHead branch (Struct/Union/EnumKeyword-led → the unified c25 structSpec/unionSpec/enumSpec defines the type via `definesWhenChild`, minting the tag into the ENCLOSING BLOCK scope), takes NO declarator, and ends at EndStatement. A NON-defining no-declarator local (`int;`) parses + types but FAILS LOUD at HIR lowering (S_DeclarationDeclaresNothing) — the SAME tier + diagnostic as the top-level no-declarator path (CST→HIR's lowerVarLikeInto: no named declarator AND no composite specifier in the head ⇒ declares nothing). identVarDecl's list is deliberately NOT made optional (a bare typedef-name `T;` must stay an expression statement, NOT a no-declarator decl); only the keyword/specifier-led varDecl needs it (the struct/union/enum definition is keyword-led).

identVarDecl.$comment: The bare-typedef-name declaration statement (`T x, *p;`). The PreferType commit guard (bare-string form — the FC2 triage): sketch-known Type commits, known Value rolls back (`a * b;` stays a multiplication), unknown runs the follower-operator test (`T x;` commits — `x` continues no value reading; `a * b;` rolls back — `*` does) and records the cross-file-oracle candidate on rollback.

declOrExprStmt.$comment: The C statement ambiguity site: FC5 prepends labelStmt (`Identifier Colon …` — a 2-token commit point disjoint from identVarDecl/exprStmt, neither of which has Colon in 2nd position), then identVarDecl BEFORE exprStmt (declared probe order is authoritative — the FC4 c0 fix). lookahead 256 (= 4096-token probe budget, vs the operand alt's 64): the identVarDecl probe must swallow an entire declaration INCLUDING initializer expressions and brace-init lists before its frame closes.

gotoStmt.$comment: D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO: `goto label;` (C 6.8.6.1) OR the GNU extension `goto *expr;` (indirect branch to a computed code address). Two alternatives, mirroring sizeofExpr's 2-alt triage. The leading `*` is consumed as GOTO-STATEMENT syntax — so `gotoIndirect`'s operand is the pointer expression `expr` itself (NOT a deref), distinct from the prec-90 prefix `*`. `gotoLabel` keeps the FC5 plain form; the label-vs-indirect choice is unambiguous on the token AFTER `goto` (Identifier+EndStatement is a label; StarOp begins an indirect target). Both lower in cst_to_hir (gotoLabel→GotoStmt, gotoIndirect→IndirectGotoStmt).

labelStmt.$c31Comment: D-CSUBSET-LABEL-BUDGET-CLIFF (p19 Cluster G c31): the shape STAYS `Identifier Colon statement` so a label stays correct in every context, incl. a braceless control-flow body (`if(x) L: s;` — `s` IS the if's body). `commitAfterPrefix` is a PEG cut: after `Identifier Colon` no other declOrExprStmt alt (identVarDecl/exprStmt — neither has Colon in 2nd position) can match, so once the rule's fixed leading token-prefix is consumed in the probe (predictivePrefixLen = 3: `Identifier`, `Colon`, plus the first `statement` token — one harmless extra token, still far under budget) the parser COMMITS and parses the (arbitrarily large) `statement` child NON-speculatively, off the lookahead*16 probe budget. Without it a labeled statement >4096 tokens (sqlite3.c:213922 `json_parse_restart:` before a ~900-case switch) exhausts the budget → rollback → exprStmt → P0001 'got :'.

localDeclSpecifier.$comment: Local storage-class specifiers + attributes. `static` (D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC) confers STATIC storage duration (C 6.2.4) — the varDecl row's linkageSpecifiers maps it to {binding:local, staticStorage:true} and CST→HIR routes the declaration to a hidden module-global; register/auto parse and are semantically inert (C: hints/default storage). `static` in a for-init is a C 6.8.5p3 violation, gated loud by the forDecl row.

param.$comment: FC4 c1: head + OPTIONAL declarator (named `int v`, pointer `const char *s`, abstract `int` / `int *` / `int (*)(int)`). The head admits a lone Identifier — C23 semantics: a lone identifier in param position is ALWAYS a type name (K&R identifier-lists were removed), so `int f(T)` is an unnamed param of type T (S_UnknownType when T isn't one).

statement.$c30Comment: c30 D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-TYPEDEF: `typedefDecl` is admitted as a block item (a BLOCK-SCOPED typedef — `typedef void(*LOGFUNC_t)(void*,int,const char*);` inside a function, sqlite3.c:187603). FIRST = TypedefKeyword, DISJOINT from every other statement alt (varDecl is qualifier/type-keyword/storage-class-led; declOrExprStmt is Identifier-led; the rest are their own keywords), so a unique-production DIRECT DESCENT — NO speculation. The whole typedef-name machinery is ALREADY scope-keyed: Pass-1 binds the alias into `current` (= the enclosing block scope) in the Ordinary namespace; the type resolver (`resolveTypeNodeImpl` SE5 arm) and the parse-time oracle (`BinderSketch`) both walk the scope chain, so a block-scoped typedef-name resolves IN the block, SHADOWS an outer same-name typedef, and does NOT leak out (red-on-disable pins enforce the non-leak). HIR→MIR's `lowerStmtNode` lowers a statement-position TypeDecl as a NO-OP (a typedef emits no code; mirrors the TOP-LEVEL TypeDecl). Mirrors c28 (block-scoped struct/union/enum definition).

forStmt.$comment: FC4 c1: the init clause mirrors the statement-level decl split — committed keyword/qualifier/specifier-led forDecl (non-speculative) vs the identifier-led forInitAmbig probe. Both are semicolon-LESS (the for grammar owns the `;`).

sehTryStmt.$comment: c115 (SEH arc 2/3): MSVC `__try compound-statement (__except '(' expr ')' compound-statement | __finally compound-statement)` — learn.microsoft.com/cpp/cpp/try-except-statement. FIRST = {TryKeyword}, disjoint from every other statement alt -> unique-production direct descent, NON-speculative; the except/finally fork is disambiguated one level down by sehHandler's own disjoint FIRSTs (ExceptKeyword vs FinallyKeyword). Both bodies REUSE the `block` rule — MSVC requires compound statements (braces), so the grammar enforces it by construction. The filter expression is an ordinary `expression`; its special context (where _exception_code()/_exception_info() are legal) is a SEMANTIC property checked at CST->HIR lowering + the HIR verifier, not a grammar one. __finally parses (so the diagnostic is a positioned lowering fail-loud citing D-CSUBSET-SEH-FINALLY, not a raw P0001 cascade) but has NO consumer (sqlite ground-truth: zero uses) — trigger-gated.

sehLeaveStmt.$comment: c115: `__leave ;` parses (FIRST = {LeaveKeyword}, disjoint) so the diagnostic is a positioned lowering fail-loud citing D-CSUBSET-SEH-LEAVE rather than a parse cascade. NO consumer (sqlite: zero uses) — trigger-gated; the lowering rejects it.

assignmentExpr.$comment: FC5: a C `assignment-expression` — every operator EXCEPT the comma (the two-level expression gate). minPrecedence 15 folds assignment (prec 15) and everything tighter but STOPS before the comma operator (prec 10, looser), so a `,` at a separator site (argList / initValue / enumerator) is left for the surrounding rule's separator, never swallowed as a comma-operator. The top-level `expression` rule (minPrec 0) keeps the comma operator for expr-statements / for-clauses / return / parens / index / conditions.

operand.$lookaheadComment: FC2 Part A: raised 8 -> 64. The speculation budget is lookahead*16 tokens per probe (parser SpeculationProbe); a castExpr probe must consume the ENTIRE cast operand expression before its frame closes, so the old 128-token budget was a correctness cliff for casts of long expressions (and for large compound-literal brace inits). 64 -> 1024-token probe budget.

compoundLiteralExpr.$comment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF): the type position is castTypeRef (typedef-admitting via its bare-Identifier base alt) with the SAME commitRequiresTypeName binder triage castExpr uses. The `{` follower makes most forms unambiguous (triage case 4: BlockOpen is not a continuable value-reading operator -> COMMIT for unknown names); a KNOWN-value identifier `(a){...}` rolls back to the value reading, whose `(a)` parenExpr then hits the orphan `{` -> LOUD parse error (C says the form is invalid anyway).

sizeofExpr.$comment: FC6: `sizeof ( type-name )` | `sizeof unary-expression` (C 6.5.3.4). The type form reuses FC2's castTypeRef + commitRequiresTypeName binder triage — `sizeof(int)` / `sizeof(struct S)` / `sizeof(char*)` commit as types; `sizeof(x)` for a value `x` rolls back to the value form (a parenExpr operand); `sizeof x` (no parens) is the value form directly. The result type is size_t (U64); the operand is UNEVALUATED — only its type reaches the SizeOf node.

labelAddressExpr.$comment: D-CSUBSET-COMPUTED-GOTO: the GNU `&&label` label-address expression. A DEDICATED operand rule (parallel to sizeofExpr) — NOT the prec-90 prefix `&` doubled — because its operand is a LABEL name in the label namespace, NOT a value: routing it through the ordinary identifier-operand path would resolve `label` as an (undefined) variable and fail to parse. The `Identifier` here is a RAW token (like labelStmt's), recovered by the CST→HIR lowering which resolves it to the label's per-function ordinal and stamps the result `void*`. The leading `&&` is the single AndAndOp token (no lexer change).

vaStartExpr.$comment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): `va_start(ap, last)`. Takes the `va_list` lvalue (assignmentExpr — the comma-free, non-comma-operator form) and an OPTIONAL second `last` arg that is parsed-and-ignored (the SysV gp/fp offsets derive from the FnSig, not from `last`). A leaf form in the operand alternative list, parallel to sizeofExpr.

vaArgExpr.$comment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): `va_arg(ap, T)`. The SECOND argument is a TYPE — it reuses FC2's shared `castTypeRef` rule + `commitRequiresTypeName` binder triage so `va_arg(ap, int)` / `va_arg(ap, MyTypedef)` commit the type position as a type (and `va_arg(ap, x)` for a VALUE x fails loud at the type-resolve, never silently treating a value as a type). The dedicated HIR lowering recovers T from the semantic stamp, exactly like sizeof.

vaEndExpr.$comment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): `va_end(ap)`. Takes the `va_list` lvalue; a no-op teardown in the SysV model.

staticAssertDecl.$comment: C11/C23 6.7.10 `_Static_assert(constant-expression, string-literal);` and the C23 1-arg `_Static_assert(constant-expression);` — a static-assertion DECLARATION valid at BOTH file scope (topLevel alt) AND block scope (statement alt). Two spellings: the reserved `_Static_assert` and the C23 `static_assert` (both keywords). The condition is `assignmentExpr` (comma-free — a `,` here separates the optional message, so the comma-operator form must not swallow it, exactly like vaStartExpr's ap arg). The message is an OPTIONAL adjacent-string-concat literal (the vaStartExpr optional-second-arg pattern; ABSENT = the C23 1-arg form). SEMANTICS live in the semantic tier (semantics.staticAssertRule): the condition is const-evaluated by the SAME evaluator that folds `sizeof(T)`/enum/arithmetic in an array dimension — ZERO ⇒ S_StaticAssertFailed (message = the string literal), NONZERO ⇒ nothing. The `hirKind: Skip` row lowers a PASSED assertion to nothing in BOTH positions (the emptyStmt/includeDirective Skip precedent), and — because it is a MAPPED rule — makes peelToCore STOP here (never descending into the sole condition child of the 1-arg form).

castTypeRef.$comment: c27 D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE: the two qualifier slots are `{repeat headQualifier}` (const|volatile, either order) — admitting a volatile-qualified cast type-name `(volatile u32 **)p` (sqlite 67392). The resolver wraps the base in VolatileQual when a volatile precedes the stars, so the cast builds Ptr<Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>> and a deref of it is volatile; const stays ignored. (Was two `{optional ConstKeyword}` slots pre-c27.) c11 D-CSUBSET-EAST-CONST-CAST: a cast/sizeof type-name admits const PRE-base (`const char *` — west) AND POST-base before the stars (`char const *` — EAST, the SQLite-pervasive form). In a DECLARATION the `*` lives in the declarator so the head `char const` already parsed; a cast bundles base+stars here, so the post-base const slot is what admits east-const. The const resolver IGNORES const for type identity (a cast yields an rvalue), so all forms intern the same pointer type. The two const slots straddle the REQUIRED base token (no ambiguity); a POST-stars qualifier (`char * const` / `u32 * volatile`) RIDES INSIDE the pointer layer (c29 D-CSUBSET-POST-STAR-CAST-QUALIFIER): the leading stars are `{repeat pointerLayer}` (= `StarOp {repeat ptrQualifier}`, c-subset ~500) NOT bare `{repeat StarOp}`, so the post-star const/volatile is present ONLY with a star — NO zero-star adjacency (the c11 ambiguity a separate post-stars optional would have caused). The resolver counts each pointerLayer child as ONE star for ptrDepth and STRIPS its ptrQualifiers: a cast yields an RVALUE with NO top-level cv (C 6.5.4) — `(int * const)p` and `(int *)p` intern the SAME `Ptr<int>`, and a post-star VOLATILE is dropped (no VolatileQual on the cast POINTER) — the c27 PRE-stars-volatile pointee path (`volatile u32 *`→`Ptr<VolatileQual(u32)>`) is SEPARATE and unbroken. c26 D-CSUBSET-ABSTRACT-DECLARATOR-TYPE-NAME: an ABSTRACT direct-declarator (`(*)(void)`/`(*)(int)`/`[N]`/`(*)[N]`) may follow the base+stars — the fn-pointer/array type-name forms (`(int(*)(void))p`, the sqlite osGeteuid cast). It reuses `abstractDirectDeclarator` (the abstract twin of `directDeclarator`, sharing parenDeclarator+fnSuffix+arrayDeclSuffix), so the semantic resolver folds the type via the SHARED `directDeclaredType` (Ptr<Fn(void)->int>) — the SAME machinery a declaration's declarator takes. It is the ABSTRACT twin (Identifier base EXCLUDED) NOT `directDeclarator` because a bare-identifier base would make `(c * c)` (a multiplication) speculatively commit as a bogus cast; and it is NOT the full `declarator` because the leading POINTER STARS are consumed by `{repeat pointerLayer}` above (a star-led declarator's FIRST `pointerLayer` would collide with the repeat — C_AmbiguousAlternatives). OPTIONAL: a star-only cast (`int*`) leaves it empty — none of {fnSuffix,parenDeclarator,arrayDeclSuffix} matches the closing `)`, so `(int*)` stays `castTypeBase`+pointerLayer(StarOp). A NAMED declarator with the name NESTED in parens (`(int (x))expr` / `(int(*p)(void))expr`) parses (via parenDeclarator) but is rejected LOUD at the type-resolve via `S_TypeNameDeclaratorNotAbstract` — never silently parsed as the bare base.

stringLiteralExpr.$comment: C 5.1.1.2 phase 6 (D-CSUBSET-ADJACENT-STRING-CONCAT): adjacent string literals concatenate — `"a" "b"` is `"ab"`. The first StringStart+StringLiteral pair is required; the zero-or-more repeat captures each further pair as FLAT children (sequences inline, no wrapper node), so a lone string yields exactly 2 children, byte-identical to the pre-c20 single-pair rule. Concatenation is at the DECODED-byte level (each body decoded by decodeStringLiteralBody FIRST, then the bytes joined) via the single decodeAdjacentStringBodies chokepoint that every body consumer routes through. Safe in every use site: primaryExpr, attr args (terminate at `)`), externFuncTail (terminates at `;`) — none has StringStart in its follow set.

argList.$comment: FC5: assignmentExpr (NOT expression) — the comma here is an arg SEPARATOR, never a comma-operator, so `f(a, b)` is 2 args. A comma-operator arg is written parenthesized: `f((a, b))`.

semantics.$volatileMarkerComment: c21 (D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-QUALIFIER): the language's volatile qualifier token. Read by the type-position resolver's CO-LOCATED pointer arm (castTypeRef cast/sizeof type-names) to reject a pointer-to-volatile-POINTEE (`volatile int *`) position-aware (volatile-before-star = pointee = REJECT; after-the-star = pointer-object = ACCEPT). DECLARATION forms — objects, params, AND struct/union members (c23 removed the head star from typeRefAllowingStruct, so a member's `*` now binds per-declarator like every other declaration position) — reject via the per-declaration-rule volatileMarker (head-volatile + a declarator star); the two arms together make the fail-loud complete.
semantics.$externLibraryByFormatComment: FF6 Slice 2 + audit fold (2026-06-02): per-format runtime library identity for source-declared externs. compileSingleUnit step 2.5 populates HirAttribute<FfiMetadata> per ExternFunction/ExternGlobal by calling synthesizeFfiFromSourceDecls() with the entry whose key matches objectFormatKindName(format.kind()). Per-LANGUAGE field (not per-declaration-rule) — the post-fold #1 placement on DeclarationRule was fragile; the pipeline first-match-loop would silently pick the wrong rule on grammars with multiple extern-shaped decl rules. Library identities: PE → msvcrt.dll (Win32 C runtime for x86_64 hello-world); ELF → libc.so.6 (glibc on Linux); Mach-O → /usr/lib/libSystem.B.dylib (libSystem dispatcher publishes the libc surface). Per-symbol overrides (`extern "otherlib.dll" int foo();`) anchored D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-LIBRARY-SYNTAX.
semantics.$shippedLibDirsComment: FF11 (2026-06-05) + Model 3 (2026-06-09): the SYSTEM include search path (C's /usr/include analogue) for the angle form `#include <h>`. A directory under src/dss-config/; the header stem maps to a language-NEUTRAL JSON descriptor (`<stem>.json`, e.g. stdio.json) read by src/ffi/shipped_lib_descriptor.cpp — its symbols (name + hir-text signature) are injected into semantic scope before Pass 2, then flow through FF5 synthesizeFfiFromSourceDecls like a program's own extern. Model 3 made each descriptor PLATFORM-NEUTRAL: ONE descriptor per header, carrying a per-object-format `library` map ({"pe":...,"elf":...,"macho":...}); the active compilation target's format selects its runtime image at compile_pipeline resolution (keyed via objectFormatKindName), so a SINGLE neutral directory serves every target. This DISSOLVED the former per-platform-directory selection + D-FFI-SHIPPED-LIB-PLATFORM-SELECT. The full surface (stdio/stdlib/string/ctype/math) + provenance/variadic-exclusion notes live in src/dss-config/shippedLibs/README.md.
semantics.$pointerConversionsComment: D-LANG-POINTER-VOID-CONVERT (step 13.2, 2026-06-02): C-standard §6.3.2.3 — `void*` converts implicitly to/from any object-pointer type without a cast. The two directions are configured INDEPENDENTLY (matching C++'s split, where only the safe direction is implicit). c-subset declares BOTH true (C-family semantics). A future C++ frontend would declare {implicitToVoidPtr: true, implicitFromVoidPtr: false} matching ISO C++ §7.11. Rust/Swift would declare both false. DEFAULT is false (struct-init); strict typing is the safety-respecting default, opposite of the Array→Ptr decay opt-out direction (which is pervasive C-family idiom; void-ptr conversion is a safety relaxation). Pointer arithmetic on void* and void*↔fn-pointer conversions are separate concerns anchored at D-LANG-VOIDPTR-ARITH-REJECT and D-LANG-VOIDPTR-FN-CONVERT respectively.
semantics.$pointerAliasingComment: Two orthogonal C99-derived alias-analysis opt-ins. `strictAliasingOnDistinctTypes` (C99 §6.5): accessing an object through a pointer of an incompatible type is undefined behavior — lets the MIR optimizer's CSE/LICM Load admission prove Ptr<I32> and Ptr<I64> don't alias (Rule 6 in mirMayAlias). `charTypesAliasAll` (C99 §6.5 ¶7): character-type pointers may alias an object of any type — the canonical strict-aliasing carve-out for serializers/hash-visitors/memcpy. C-family languages declare both true. A Rust frontend or strict-typed DSL would declare `charTypesAliasAll: false` (the type-checker enforces aliasing at a higher tier). See D-OPT-LOAD-ALIAS-ANALYSIS for the substrate's design.
semantics.$declaratorsComment: FC4 c1 stage 2a: the C 6.7.6 declarator role vocabulary. The shared declarator walk (name extraction — binder sketch + analyzer) and the Pass 1.5 inversion engine consume ONLY these resolved roles. The pointer/fn/array/group structure lives in the DECLARATOR; heads carry the base type alone.
semantics.$parametersComment: C 6.7.6.3p10: a parameter list of exactly `(void)` declares ZERO parameters. The engine applies this at the ONE param-harvest chokepoint when the sole param resolves to lattice Void AND is unnamed; a NAMED void param or void-mixed-with-others is S_InvalidVoidParam. Languages without the flag keep raw param lists.

declarations.$prototypeSynthesizesExternComment: c86 (D-CSUBSET-BARE-PROTO-EXTERN-SYNTHESIS): a BLOCK-scope bare prototype (`int f(int);` inside a body — re-homed onto the file scope per D-CSUBSET-BLOCK-SCOPE-PROTOTYPE, C 6.2.2p4) that survives with no in-TU definition synthesizes the same no-library extern as a file-scope proto (routed to the module decls, mirroring the static-local accumulator). Same three-way resolution as topLevelDecl's flag.
declarations.$staticStorageComment: D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC: a block-scope `static` confers static storage duration (C 6.2.4). CST→HIR's linkageFrom folds {binding:local, staticStorage:true} from the SAME prefix scan and routes the declaration to a hidden module-global (internal linkage). register/auto/attribute specifiers carry no linkage and are ignored (ignoredKinds/ignoredRules) so they never trip the unknown-specifier diagnostic.
declarations.$volatileComment: c27 (D-CSUBSET-VOLATILE-POINTEE): `volatile` is a TYPE qualifier (TypeKind::VolatileQual). The resolver wraps the base in VolatileQual when a volatileMarker precedes the stars (`volatile int *` => Ptr<VolatileQual(int)>) and wraps the pointer for an east ptrQualifier (`int * volatile` => VolatileQual(Ptr<int>)); object-volatility is read off the symbol's top-level VolatileQual type at access lowering, pointee-volatility rides the accessed type at the deref/index/member (MirInstFlags::Volatile). ALL pointee/member/param/typedef forms now compile — the prior S_VolatilePointeeNotSupported reject is retired. const stays ignored (no wrapper).

declarations.$forStaticComment: D-CSUBSET-LOCAL-STATIC (MF-2): `static` parses in the shared localDeclSpecifiers prefix, but a static (or any non-auto/register) storage class in a for-statement init-declaration is a C 6.8.5p3 constraint violation — gate it LOUD (never silently lower as an automatic loop variable). NO linkageSpecifiers here: a static for-init must error, not route to a hidden global. The StaticKeyword gatedMarker stays; only the S_VolatileNotSupported gate is removed (volatile is implemented — c21).

declarations.$arrayToPointerComment: c82 (D-CSUBSET-PARAM-ARRAY-ADJUSTMENT, C 6.7.6.3p7): a parameter declared 'array of T' — sized (`int a[64]`), unsized (`int a[]`), or multi-dim (`int a[][5]` -> ptr to int[5]) — ADJUSTS to 'pointer to T'. The flag (a) permits the absent-length `[]` on the param's OUTERMOST suffix (resolving via the incomplete-array path, like allowFlexibleArray) and (b) rewrites the resolved top-level Array to Ptr<element> at BOTH resolution sites (the definitive Pass-1.5 visit that binds the param symbol — so sizeof(param)==8 and body uses see the pointer — and the FnSig param harvest — so call-site checking matches the decayed argument). An inner-dimension `[]` (`int a[4][]`) still fails loud (S_FlexibleArrayInAggregate via the incomplete-element guard), matching C's ill-formedness.

declarations.$prototypeSynthesizesExternComment: c86 (D-CSUBSET-BARE-PROTO-EXTERN-SYNTHESIS, C 6.2.2p5): a file-scope bare prototype with NO in-TU definition (`void sqlite3_free(void*);` — the sqlite3.h splice shape; SQLITE_API expands empty) synthesizes an ExternFunction with NO library binding. The LK11 merge resolves it against a sibling TU's definition (import stripped, calls direct); a bare re-declaration of a SHIPPED descriptor name re-binds to the descriptor's library (goal-2 suppression recorded the map); a survivor with neither fails LOUD at link (K_SymbolUndefined naming the symbol — ld's behavior). `static`/weak protos never synthesize (internal/weak linkage must not bind a sibling TU's public symbol) — they keep the loud H0009. RED-ON-DISABLE: drop this flag -> the shell.c 2-TU probe regresses to H0009 'Ref to unbound symbol' at the first sqlite3_* call.
declarations.$linkageSpecifiersComment: D-CSUBSET-LINKAGE-SPECIFIERS: 'static' -> internal (Local) binding; '__attribute__((weak))' -> Weak; FC4 c1 adds the COMPOSITE key 'visibility:hidden' — linkageFrom forms '<identifier>:<decoded-string-body>' when a specifier identifier is immediately followed by a parenthesized string literal (__attribute__((visibility("hidden")))). 'linkageSpecifierIgnoredRules' skips C23 [[...]] stdAttr subtrees wholesale (D14: parsed, ignored).

kindByChild.$comment: Post-strip children: [topLevelHead, initDeclaratorList, topLevelDeclTail]. childPath [2,0] = the tail's sole child — a `block` node (function definition) or the EndStatement token (variable; whenRule never matches a token). paramsPath/bodyPath are ABSENT: in declarator mode the params live inside the declarator's fnSuffix and the matched block IS the body.

declarations.$implicitReturnZeroForFunctionNamesComme: C99 §5.1.2.2.3: when control reaches the closing '}' of main without an explicit return, the semantics are equivalent to 'return 0;'. The HIR lowering appends a synthetic ReturnStmt(IntLiteral(0)) to main's body if it doesn't structurally terminate. SOURCE-AGNOSTIC: this list lives on the per-language semantic config so each language declares its own entry-fn convention (Pascal's 'program', Rust's 'fn main', etc.) without touching shared HIR substrate. Closes D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN.
declarations.$entryFunctionNamesComment: FC5: the program ENTRY-point name(s) — read by the driver's entry-symbol resolution. SEPARATE from implicitReturnZeroForFunctionNames (the C main-style return-0 set); for C both are `main`, but declaring them distinctly de-conflates the two concepts (D-LK10-ENTRY-MAIN-IMPLICIT-RETURN).

declarations.$c25Comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the DEFINITION arm of the unified `structSpec`. `definesWhenChild: structBody` makes this row a definition (mint type, bind tag in the Tag namespace, open scope, compose fields) ONLY when a `structBody` child is present (`struct S { … }` / anonymous `struct { … }`); when ABSENT (`struct S v;` / `struct S;`) the node is a pure tag reference, resolved by the paired `structSpec` references-row below (isTagReference). The DEFINE semantics are byte-identical to the former `structSpecifierBody` row — fields now live under the `structBody` child, but Pass-1.5's fieldChildren collection walks the scope this row opens (not direct children), so the extra nesting is transparent. `name: 1` still resolves the tag: structSpec's visible children at definition are [StructKeyword, Identifier, structBody] (tagged) or [StructKeyword, structBody] (anonymous → anonymousNameAllowed synthesizes the name).
c25: the DEFINITION arm of the unified `unionSpec` (mirrors structSpec). `definesWhenChild: unionBody`.

c25: the DEFINITION arm of the unified `enumSpec` (mirrors structSpec). `definesWhenChild: enumBody`. The enumerator lift-to-enclosing-scope behavior is unchanged (fieldChildren.liftToEnclosingScope).

declarations.$allowFlexibleArrayComment: c82 (D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-INCOMPLETE-ARRAY, C 6.7p7/6.9.2): an extern OBJECT declaration may carry an INCOMPLETE array type — sqlite3.h's `SQLITE_EXTERN const char sqlite3_version[];`. The absent-length `[]` resolves through the SAME incomplete-array path a FAM uses (interner incompleteArray = the Array kind + a length sentinel, so decay/index consumers keep working while sizeof fails loud). The symbol is non-defining: a sibling-TU DEFINITION completes it at the MIR merge (LK11 name resolution drops the import row); a NEVER-defined one survives as an extern-data import, which the link tier fail-louds on until the extern-data import model lands (the c82 §B, D-LK-EXTERN-DATA-IMPORT).

references.$c25Comment: c25 D-CSUBSET-UNIFIED-COMPOSITE-SPECIFIER: the REFERENCE arm of the unified specifiers. A body-ABSENT `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` (`struct S v;` / `struct S;`) is a TAG reference — these rows resolve its tag in the Tag namespace, exactly as the former `structTypeRef`/etc. did, via the SAME isTagReference path. The DEFINITION arm (body present) is the paired declarations-row above; the engine routes per-node on the body child's presence (`definesWhenChild`), so a definition is never double-processed as a reference. `nameMatch: lastIdentifier` extracts the tag from [Kw, Identifier]. c35 (D-CSUBSET-FORWARD-STRUCT-DECLARATION): `compositeKind` drives the forward-mint of an INCOMPLETE composite on a tag MISS (an opaque `struct S *` / `typedef struct S S;` whose S is never defined — sqlite3_stmt), so the reference resolves to a sizeable Ptr<incomplete> instead of S_UnknownType; a VALUE/by-value-member/sizeof of it still fails loud (computeLayout incomplete guard). `enum` keeps the fail-loud miss (value-typed).

semantics.$scopesComment: FC4 c1: topLevelDecl replaced funcDefTail — a function's params now live INSIDE its declarator's fnSuffix, so the scope that holds them must dominate BOTH the declarator and the block; the topLevelDecl node is that dominator (harmless for variable-kind topLevelDecls — nothing binds into the scope). c25: the unified `structSpec`/`unionSpec`/`enumSpec` rules are the fieldChildren composites (they REPLACE the retired *SpecifierBody rules). Each opens a scope ONLY in DEFINITION mode (body present) — Pass 1 gates the pushScope on the `definesWhenChild` body-child presence, so a body-absent tag reference (`struct S v;`) opens no scope. The member-body wrapper rules (structBody/unionBody/enumBody) are deliberately NOT scope rules — their fields bind into the enclosing XxxSpec scope.
semantics.$castsComment: FC2: explicit C cast `(T)expr`. typeChild/operandChild are visible-child indices into castExpr = [ '(' , castTypeRef , ')' , castOperand ]. Pass 2 resolves+stamps the type child (typedefs incl. cross-tree-injected ones resolve through the standard type-position resolver), stamps the cast node's RESULT type, and validates the pair against the explicit-cast matrix (scalar<->scalar incl. float<->int, ptr<->ptr, ptr<->int; struct values / void / arrays -> S_InvalidCast).
semantics.$sizeofComment: FC6: sizeof typing. sizeofType = [ SizeofKeyword, '(', castTypeRef, ')' ] → typeChild 2 is the castTypeRef (resolved + stamped through the SAME type resolver casts use). sizeofValue = [ SizeofKeyword, castOperand ] (the operand is typed normally). Both forms are stamped size_t (U64).
semantics.$variadicComment: FC12a-core (D-FC12A-VARIADIC-CALLEE): variadic-intrinsic typing. vaArgExpr = [ VaArgKeyword, '(', assignmentExpr(apChild 2), ',', castTypeRef(typeChild 4), ')' ] → pass 2 resolves+stamps the castTypeRef through the SAME resolver casts use (so the HIR lowering recovers the read type T) and stamps the node T; it type-checks `ap` is a va_list. vaStartExpr/vaEndExpr = [ kw, '(', assignmentExpr(apChild 2), ... ] → node stamped `void`; ap type-checked va_list; vaStart also flips the enclosing function's uses-va-start attribute so the LIR prologue spills the arg registers.
semantics.$staticAssertComment: C11/C23 6.7.10: the `_Static_assert`/`static_assert` DECLARATION rule. Pass 2 const-evaluates the condition child (visible-child 1 — the assignmentExpr after the keyword+'(') through the SAME evaluator that folds sizeof(T)/enum/arithmetic in an array dimension; ZERO ⇒ S_StaticAssertFailed (message = the OPTIONAL trailing string-literal child), NONZERO ⇒ nothing. The construct lowers to nothing (hirLowering staticAssertDecl → Skip).
semantics.$compoundLiteralsComment: FC3.5 sweep-c3 (D-CSUBSET-COMPOUND-LITERAL-TYPEDEF): the compound-literal type position resolves + stamps through the SAME type-position resolver casts use (typedefs included). typeChild is the visible-child index into compoundLiteralExpr = [ '(' , castTypeRef , ')' , braceInitList ]. NO operandChild / cast-matrix validation — a compound literal is C 6.5.2.5 postfix syntax, not a conversion.
semantics.$builtinFunctionsComment: c103 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-UMULH): compiler intrinsics exposed as builtin functions (SE6 builtins scope, shadow-able by user decls). `__umulh` = the high 64 bits of the u64*u64 128-bit product (the MSVC/sqlite intrinsic used by sqlite3Multiply128/160 on _WIN64). `lowering:umulh` binds the call to a DEDICATED MIR op (x86 `mul r/m64` -> RDX, arm64 `umulh`) rather than an ordinary call/import -- a compiler intrinsic is not a linkable symbol. Available on every target that declares the umulh mnemonic vocabulary (x86_64 + arm64); a target lacking it fails loud at lowering.
semantics.$builtinAtomicCasComment: c104 (D-CSUBSET-INTRINSIC-ATOMIC-CAS): `_InterlockedCompareExchange(LONG volatile* dest, LONG exch, LONG comp) -> LONG` -- the MSVC atomic compare-and-swap intrinsic (returns the ORIGINAL *dest; stores exch iff original==comp). Win32 LONG = i32 (LLP64) -> the 32-bit CAS. The UNDERSCORE name is the real intrinsic exactly as the Windows SDK spells it; windows.json's `InterlockedCompareExchange` macro expands to it (the winnt.h convention), so sqlite's `#if defined(InterlockedCompareExchange)` takes the direct-intrinsic path and its calls land here. `signature` (full type-text, c104) instead of scalar params: the dest param is a POINTER. Lowers to x86 `lock cmpxchg` (implicit-RAX comparand/old) / an arm64 LDAXR-STLXR acquire-release loop.
semantics.$builtinTypesComment: FC3 c1: the TEXT-keyed type-name table (typedef-style positions + the load-time fallback for single-word ladder/promotion names). The KEYWORD grammar path resolves through semantics.typeSpecifiers below (the C 6.7.2 multiset table) — both tables declare the SAME widths, and `long` carries the LLP64 override on BOTH so the two paths can never disagree (D-LANG-PLATFORM-DEPENDENT-PRIMITIVE-WIDTH).
semantics.$typeSpecifiersComment: FC3 c1: every VALID C type-specifier multiset (C 6.7.2 6) over the shipped keyword surface; combinations are ORDER-FREE (the engine canonicalizes by sorting token kinds; `long long` = LongKeyword twice). Invalid combos (`unsigned float`, `short long`, `long double`) reject by ABSENCE -> S_InvalidTypeSpecifierCombination. `long`-bearing rows carry the LLP64 (Windows) 32-bit override; `long long` stays 64-bit everywhere. NO `long double` row: F128/long-double is an FC3+ exclusion (rejects by absence).
semantics.$literalTypesComment: FC3 c1: IntLiteral's I32 is the BASE core; the integerLiteralTyping ladder below refines the width by magnitude/suffix/radix per C 6.4.4.1. FloatLiteral's F64 is the BASE core; the floatLiteralTyping suffix map below refines it per C 6.4.4.2 (FC3.5 sweep-c2 / D-CSUBSET-F32-CODEGEN closure: `1.5f` now types F32 — the F32 arithmetic surface shipped, so the typing flip pairs with real codegen). true/false are C23 keyword literals: type Bool with config-declared FIXED values (1/0) — the engine never decodes their text as a number.
semantics.$charStringLiteralComment: C 6.4.4.4: a character constant has type `int` (so sizeof('c')==4, NOT 1) — CharLiteral→I32. C 6.4.5: a string literal has type char[N+1] — StringLiteral declares stringArray:true with element core Char, so the engine builds Array<Char, decodedLen+1> per occurrence (the HIR value path independently lowers the same Array<Char,N+1> via the shared char_decode). charConvertsToArith (below) admits the C char<->int implicit conversion in BOTH directions: int->char (`char x='c';`) and char->int widening (`int y=c;`; codegen materializes the Char->int SExt). Pinned by CharParamReturnedAsIntIsClean + the char_value corpus.
semantics.$intCrossSignednessComment: C 6.3.1.3/6.5.16.1 (D-CSUBSET-INT-CROSS-SIGNEDNESS-CONVERT): admit the signed<->unsigned implicit assignment conversion (`int x=u;`, `u=i;`, `return i;`/`f(u)` across the signedness boundary) — value-preserving in range, modular out of range; needed for full-C + SQLite's int/unsigned/size_t mixing. coerce()'s arithmetic-core arm materializes the width-exact Cast. SAME-signedness narrowing (`int x=aLong`) is the SIBLING gate intSameSignednessNarrows below.
semantics.$intSameSignNarrowComment: C 6.3.1.3/6.5.16.1 (D-CSUBSET-INT-SAME-SIGN-NARROW): admit the same-signedness integer NARROWING implicit conversion (`short s=anInt;`, `signed char c=anInt;`, `int i=aLong;` across init/assign/call-arg/return) — value-preserving in range, truncating (modular) out of range; needed for SQLite, which narrows everywhere. The isAssignable rank arms admit rank(rhs)>rank(lhs) only when true; WIDENING stays unconditional. coerce()'s arithmetic-core arm materializes the width-exact Cast (MIR Trunc) — the SAME path cross-signedness narrowing already uses, so NO codegen change. Together with intCrossSignednessConverts this completes the C integer-conversion matrix.
semantics.$intFloatConvertComment: C 6.3.1.4/6.3.1.5/6.5.16.1 (D-CSUBSET-INT-FLOAT-CONVERSION): admit the int<->float implicit assignment conversion in BOTH directions, gated independently. intConvertsToFloat -> an integer flows into a float lhs (`double d=5;`, `f(anInt)` to a `double` param; the sqlite kahanBabuskaNeumaierStep(pSum,iBig) shape feeds an i64 to a `volatile double`). floatConvertsToInt -> a float flows into an integer lhs (`int n=aDouble;`, truncating toward zero). coerce()'s arithmetic-core arm materializes the MIR SIToFP/UIToFP (int->float) / FPToSI/FPToUI (float->int). Flipping either to false -> that direction errors S0003 S_TypeMismatch (the int_float_convert corpus is RED-ON-DISABLE). Completes the C arithmetic-conversion matrix alongside intCrossSignednessConverts + intSameSignednessNarrows.
semantics.$floatLiteralTypingComment: FC3.5 sweep-c2: C 6.4.4.2 — a floating constant's type is keyed by its SUFFIX alone (unsuffixed -> double, f/F -> float). Type names resolve through typeSpecifiers/builtinTypes at LOAD time like the integer ladder's candidates. The loader cross-checks every numberStyle.floatSuffixes spelling is covered exactly once + an unsuffixed rule exists. C's l/L long-double suffix is NOT declared in floatSuffixes (pre-existing FC1c2 note) — long double lands with its own width story, not here.
semantics.$integerLiteralTypingComment: FC3 c1: C 6.4.4.1 EXACTLY — the FIRST candidate (per suffix-group x radix-class) whose range holds the decoded magnitude is the literal's type. `decimal` = no declared numberStyle prefix matched; `nondecimal` (hex/octal/binary) adds the unsigned candidates per the C table. Type names resolve through typeSpecifiers/builtinTypes at LOAD time, so LLP64's 32-bit `long` falls out of the data-model select (2147483648 is `long` on LP64 but `long long` on LLP64). Suffix GROUPS list the exact numberStyle.integerSuffixes spellings they cover; the loader cross-checks every declared suffix is covered exactly once. A magnitude beyond the last candidate -> S_IntegerLiteralTooLarge.
semantics.$arithmeticConversionsComment: FC3 c1: C 6.3.1.8 usual arithmetic conversions, declared (the engine algorithm in analysis/semantic/type_rules.hpp is closed-verb config-driven; languages WITHOUT this block keep the legacy commonType path). integerPromotion: operands below `int`'s rank promote to int; `alsoPromote` pulls char/bool (outside the width-rank lattice) into promotion WITHOUT the engine hardcoding C's view of char. mixedSignedness `rank-prefer-unsigned`: unsigned rank >= signed rank -> the unsigned type, else the (strictly wider) signed type. promoteComparisons: comparisons convert operands to the common type (so -1 > 0ul compares as U64 -> true) and still yield Bool. shiftResult `promotedLeft` (C 6.5.7): a shift's type is its PROMOTED LEFT operand (the right operand never contributes, so `i32 << i64` is I32) -- the engine reads this verb instead of hardcoding C's special shift rule; the alternative `commonType` types `<<`/`>>` like an ordinary binary op (common type of both).
